reviews.review_description:
(Listen before buy) okay so this game is not good let’s break it down 


1 the game haves so many cutscene and 1 mission literally is just talking for like 30 minutes and you can’t do free roam while battling 


2 no multiplayer it’s only single player so that halfway worse


3 the game took me like an hour of finish with half of it just yapping AIs so I don’t recommd you buying this game literally trash

There were people on this project who loved what they were doing.
There were people on the project and it was there first time, or they were bitter or angry or something.
The game gives you opportunities to enjoy the world, then literally after 3 seconds, has voices rushing you, every 1 second. Like literally no pause. How did no one catch that?
That writing is subpar. It starts strong then just sink spirals.
There’s a lot  thrown onto the wall here, with most of it good, if not them throwing a lot over a little time.

There is A LOT of potential here, but again. Someone in charge made some very VERY questionable decisions.

It's worth the 5 dollars but not worth it for the full price, it's not a game, it's an interactive movie, you pull levers and that's it, wanna know the endings? Repeat the last chapter, you do not fully control it. If you want experience just watch the YT vids or if it goes $5.

reviews.review_title: It’s cool but it needs a lot to it
reviews.review_description:
Make a lot of parts for this game because this is a game that needs more too it like can you add more part and more updates that would be so cool if you could like you now where some games say to be continued you need to really add a hole lot to it I love it but this game really needs more so please make this game the best gundam game it is don’t take it personal but make it where it is like a lot of endless chapters and series and acts please 
Please please I won’t stop playing this game until it has a lot of chapters series and acts please make it an endless game don’t let it go into the shadows please it has to continue so please read this and do it please make it possible to do I’m begging you and add new missions that have the word new at the top right side of the screen and add even more missions new ones and  make it where you can drive other gundams I’m begging you
The addition of Quest Achievements brought me back! What an awesome interactive movie, and a unique Achievement Hunt!

this game was honestly pretty great i'm making this that's why they can make more chapters for the layer future so if they can even make that possible i would really hope to see more chapter so please make more chapters i'm begging you please please please please
Could use some more optimization, a lot of times my hands would stop gripping the controls, especially in Arena

It was I very unique and immersive game but I very much dislike how short it was as well as the ending because i didn’t enjoy how we just let her die and not save her because i know that in gundam the main character would have done so plus there was a perfect chance for a romance between the two characters and seeing him let her die just feels wrong and makes the game too short and not worth the $18 I spent so please consider my request to add more to the game as well as a love story between the main character and the girl you could even make it an option like before starting the game you choose which path to take and thank you for reading my response.

reviews.review_title: Waited so long for this moment
reviews.review_description: Huge Gundam wing fan and Gundam fan in general! This title is as described and genuinely good and enjoyable. Wish Gundam would come out with a "Gundam" game similar to "Underdogs" but with out having to be a sandbox or using arms for locomotion. Please make more games for VR ! This game is a tease ! Still great but please make more. If you put out a VR cockpit Gundam wing game I would buy it in a heart beat I don't care if it cost $60. Just to pilot the deathscythe , heavy arms, sandrock, wing gundam, tall geese , Epyon, etc would be so official!!!! Seriously gundam wing was the most successful gundam series in the USA please make a gundam wing title.

The game constantly forces you to press buttons just for the sake of being interactive but the controls are terrible and make no sense why do I need to move the trottle up and down to fire my gun?!? I have a trigger on my joystick just let me fire by pulling the trigger! The worst part is it keeps letting go of the controls and you just lose control all the time for no reason. This would be better as a non-interactive cinematic experience. The controls would need to be completly overhauled to make this a decent playable game.

The game was amazing to me but it kinda left out the feeling of piloting a mobile suit I wish the game was open world and we could actually do more with piloting the mobile suit instead of having various action scenes I would love if there was a game mode that would be unlocked for that after the story
Overall this game was ok but there was parts that I felt unsatisfied with

reviews.review_title: Bought to see full-size Gundams, can't
reviews.review_description: The story was fine enough, but I bought it so I could see full sized mobile suits. Every time I try and launch the mixed reality gallery, it crashes with an out of memory error on my Quest 2 headset. Disappointed, to say the least.
This game is amazing I fell in love with gundam because of this game its so worth buying

To be fair and give credit where credit is due the story is amazing and this is supposed to be an interactive VR "movie" not a game. On the other hand (spoiler) you only get to use the Gundam's true power once, not in a full fight just in a singular interaction. There's really not enough fighting when there could be and while for a lot of games a major issue is how much things are repeated but in this it's the other way around, something will be introduced and then you'll only use it 2-4 times. Once again, amazing story tho and an appearance of my GLORIOUS GOAT Amuro Ray.
I love gundam. But holy crap this was MID. The story felt super short. The gameplay was boring and super hand holding. Overall its not bad (IG) BUT it's still a little disappointing

we need chinese subtitle!
we need chinese subtitle!
we need chinese subtitle!

There're a lot of times where instead of getting out of first person perspective we're instead watching from the cameraman perspective, which, while not horrible, the entire scene could've worked perfectly fine remaining in the MC's perspective.
Other aspects such as combat are okay, would appreciate using more of the quest controller buttons and triggers, though.
Overall, it was an okay experience, could be polished more, but I'm definitely looking forward to see if there's going to be a next installment.

reviews.review_title: One of my favorite gundams now
reviews.review_description: Beautiful well made game 🙏

This is like being in a 5D movie. Soooo much better than 3D.  I finished the movie and will go though it again.

This is a movie with game play elements. Not the other way around

reviews.review_title: Buggy with some design issues, and yet..
reviews.review_description: As a Gundam fan I really wanted to like this. I expected a mostly hands off experience peppered with interactive events, and got that. But there are some annoying issues and poor design choices. To name a few: I can't exit the game, the Oculus button brings up a frozen or super-slow menu that I can't use to exit; sometimes the game shifts my position within the cockpit slightly; the subtitles are hard to read and focus eyes on; English script and voice acting are mediocre (making it consistent with most of Gundam, I suppose); and the suddenly changing camera positions during cutscenes takes some getting used to. However the music is excellent, and the renderings of characters and mechanical objects are great.

There was almost no gameplay, Even then the controls are janke, no skip able cut scenes, and the cut scenes go on for over an hour. To top it off what little gameplay there is makes you look like you have the hub up and is giving someone else a hand

If you are a Gundam fan then I would recommend but if you are not then this title will be a let down. The story was ok, be prepared for a lot of dialogue. The presentation was good and it worked well on the quest 3. The cockpit, the characters, and the visuals of the world looked good. My main problem is with the gameplay. The gameplay is very lacking, only giving you limited and very simple controls over the Gundam you are piloting. In some scenes the controls can be unresponsive and there is no clear directions on what to do during those scenes. Overall just an ok experience.

reviews.review_title: More to be desired; notes for the future
reviews.review_description:
- The main content of the game is around 2 hours of story driven content. The dialogue while voiced well is shaky, and the mechanics leave more to be desired. If you are a Gundam fan and *new* to VR this should be fun. If you are not *new* to VR it is quite disappointing as it follows a rigid story driven path that has limited control and freedom. I had a few issues in particular that I believed needed improving or revising, especially for a future title that I'll list below the tldr.
- TLDR; while engaging it lacks good mechanics in the not so engaging story that seems to have misplaced its efforts.
- Issues; 
- - My biggest issue was the choice to use vertical back and forth controls for the Gundam and not tilt and/or trigger controls.
- - The look mechanic was interesting at first but should be reconsidered as, personally, I'd rather use the motion of my controls for movement. Like tilting in a fighter jet.
- - Optimization; it was going to be on here don't kid yourselves. Constant load screens that while quick, were still constant. If games like Contractors can find a way around them I don't think it's asking much for a theatrical style media to avoid them or at least come up with a unique/smooth transition. This was very Bethesda-y.
- Personal Notes; I'm not the most driven lore seeker for Gundam so perhaps I'm not the intended audience, but as a huge VR gamer, I expect more for the cost than a mid VR experience. The bonus content is unique and cool, more Gundam-y and fan focused with unique gimmicks. Maybe it's because I'm a kit builder, but if the gameplay isn't going to be enthralling like Titanfall or armored core, which was not what I was expecting going into it, than I was hoping for some serious attention to detail befitting a that of an experience, i.e. gimmicks. GIMMICKS like activating unique modes or the core fighter or something more.
- Final Notes; Stepping away from everything I'd like an phasis on what I believe is a core part of Gundam, and that is the gimmicks/detail. I think as a starting point for exploring VR as a medium this title is fine, however, I hope that there are many, and I do mean many takeaways for what is hopefully another much better future installment. BANDAI can and has done some amazing work and each model kit is incredibly well thought out so it's my hope that the same is done for VR in the future. Thank you for your time.
Regards,
  ColateralDmg

reviews.review_title: It’s like being in the anime!!! 🔥🔥
reviews.review_description: I felt like a true gundam pilot!! It was like being in a gundam movie! The angles the story the action it was so much fun I’ve never been so immersed. If you’re even remotely a gundam fan you owe it to yourself to play this game! The mixed reality side game is also really fun a feast for gundam fans indeed!! 🔥🙏
not a game, more like a disney ride, love gundam but this feels like a lost opportunity

Absolutely love the gameplay in the animation. I say the only thing that I would say is make the story longer or maybe just add a perpetual story and a PVP mode. I basically would like a game that’s like that game in the build diver anime.￼

reviews.review_title: Brilliant for fans and curious people
reviews.review_description: Loved every second of it. The actual VR elements and interaction are a little dated, but this is a format which should be explored further by creators. Hoping for more Gundam VR experiences in the future but grateful for this.

Throughout the entire campaign, I was having a blast at the visuals and gameplay this title has to offer, and I was constantly having fun! Glad that they managed to fit this in the Universal Century (U.C.) timeline so well, and like many, I want MORE! Very unique control scheme, so it makes it feel like you’re truly flying a mobile suit. Can’t wait for any new content they’ll add in the future!!

Now, don't get me wrong. It was a great first VR Gundam game. The gameplay was okay, but it could have been better. The story was not great at all. I feel like it was cut too short and did not have a lot of meaning. not to give spoilers but the ending was not good. 

I would say if you are a fan of Gundam, get it. It is still worth a shot. If you are on the fence, just watch it on YouTube and save your money.

5/10 would recommend
Really great would love to see more shows. A great concept would be mobile suit customization and freely control the arms instead of the cockpit controls for easier movement in the arena.

So I just got Gundam Silver Phantom and I love the story but I’m having trouble with the mixed reality portion. I enabled the settings but when I went to set my room scale it freezes and moves extremely slow and movements are not precise on top of that when the option tells me to set my room scale I can’t when get my controller to point at the ok button to even start setting it up. Please fix the MR portion.

This game was clearly made by people that grew up with Gundam, the shows, the toys, the models, and partnered with people that understand spatial gaming. The storyline was fun in the way that you are in an interactive movie, less a VR game, more you control some of the cut scenes. 

The MR mini game though was AWESOME. Please keep expanding that! 

I'd love to see more use of the spatial mapping in your room, and planet based combat where gravity is present and you "remote control" the gundam around your space vs the hold it in hand your play. Both would be equally awesome, but I think the remote control version could lend it self to PvP in the future where another player could share your space and remote control their mech against you or in coop against enemy waves. The wave based combat is def the way to go because it relies less on the need for a fixed level design.

Also, I know dioramas would be super interesting for the gunpla community. Allowing them to pick and place scenery, effects, and mechs to create their own dioramas. Add in basic paint brush tools and you have a winner. Demeo did a great job with this where there is a community space that you can see other players painting in and see their creations. Something similar would be awesome to build in community around the game.

You rock, keep at it, I'll pay!

I found to be let down massively with this game. What gameplay there is really sucks and it’s insanely limited. Only a couple movements to the controls. Game is nowhere near what I hoped for. I’d give it zero stars if I could.

Completed the storyline, I feel very satisfied! I feel quite immersive in experiencing the plot! The soundtrack is also doing well! The most exciting thing is to experience combat from the cockpit, and it feels great! Really hope there are more up to the theme of VR works out! Thank you to the game production team!
This was the best immersive game I kept the language speaking by the characters Japanese as it was more authentic I did have English subtitles and it kinda made me emotional when I did ending B

Bought on release day to support the official release, and only have Maybe 30 minutes of game time at time of review. 
Booted it once quickly when it downloaded and compared to my recent session there was obviously an update that targeted ui polishing as all the windows in the terminals now pop out instead of being in the computer screens. Not sure what all updated but that was one thing and the controls to reload the Mecha gun apparently were also affected as O can not complete the tutorial. 

This is truly devestating for Bandai to have happen to their game. It’s much worse because the meta refund policy will not allow me to silently send the faulty product back to the seller. Very disheartening to know Bandai name is tied to such poor build quality. 

All assets and artwork do look very akin to the Bandai animation stamp of quality and branding, it was nice to see but without Gameplay I feel I spent $30 on a custom Oculus home.

reviews.review_title: It felt like life in an anime movie.
reviews.review_description: I loved it, not much more to say. The story obstructs your mobility, but it adds to the appreciation of anime series and movies.

While I wouldn't necessarily call this a traditional "video game", it is some sort of hybrid between watching a Gundam episode and playing a video game. It was very immersive and hard to put down. I am super glad I bought this. You simply can't have experiences like this on a flat screen.
Has to be one of the worst gundam stories I’ve seen

Love it! Great graphics and beautiful storyline i teared up had to dry my headset off. Now, to the icing on the cake, mixed reality battle gunpla in your hands. So, to everyone who had something to do with this project, thank you for bringing an anime from my childhood to reality. Bucket list check off.
story mode is mid the mixed reality mini games are the best part

reviews.review_title: A Most Play for Gundam fans!
reviews.review_description: Incredibly immersive and interesting world. Hand tracking works perfectly. Wish it was longer, but maybe we’ll get a sequel if we support this game!
Great, but Please give a gundam game to us, not just a movie

I was very hyped when I saw that a Gundam VR experience was on its way. From a visual perspective, it's amazing, with crisp details and a very anime look. It has some really neat extras, like the Hanger and some anime clips.

Unfortunately, that's about the extent of the good parts.

The story has potential but is disjointed and poorly paced. The characters have a little promise, but the finale just... ruins that. 

The first two Arcs feel like tutorials, with the last Arc being a few chapters long (chapters being small sections of action). The game feels very short for that reason. I honestly was shocked when it ended after what felt like an hour.

The controls are mixed. The visual inputs, moving your head feel okay and are somewhat interesting. But the two handles in the cockpit are horrible, frustrating, and poorly designed. Even when 'gripped' you can easily lose hold and only realise when you look down. The actual combat is... just sad really. Interesting once and then repeated the whole game.

Really, you are buying this for the extras which are fairly cool.

So much promise and yet, feels like a Tech demo with a story hacked in.

me: yesss :D
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
Die :(

reviews.review_title: A fun and easy to play Interactive Movie
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed this interactive movie. I think Gundam fans will enjoy this movie because there are a lot of references to other Gundam shows. There is also custom animated scenes that Gundam fans will enjoy. The plot is easy to follow, and the beginning of the movie introduces the Gundam story, so people new to the Gundam franchise will find this movie easy to follow.

The graphics are pretty good. Nothing spectacular, but on par with what is achievable on the Meta Quest 2. The ship and mobile suit designs are excellent. They're really showing off what Bandai can do with their 3D models.

The interactive elements in the movie are easy to follow. They're QTE style events, and point and shoot.

While the interactive elements are easy, they are a bit clunky, which is my main gripe with the game. The control to shoot when piloting the mobile suit is to move the controller forward and then back to reload. I wish they  let us pull the trigger to shoot. That would be easier and more intuitive.
Thought there would be more interaction other and just pulling 2 levers

Huge anime fan who's been waiting for solid content. 

An original mixed action experience. Controls are mediocre, but out shadowed by the clear effort to make a cohesive story you can become immersed in. This is a HUGE step in the right direction for anime content. 

For the older crowd this hits the nostalgia checkbox.

Clearly made for VR. I'm so ready for more!

The First of his Genre, and it hits the Target!
A bit boring in the middle but, great storyline overall!
Interactive level is interesting, and keeps you there and active.

MR Minigame is basic but great fun!

I really hope to see an FPS/Hack n Slash Gundam Game in the near future! (MS08th Team based would be a NUKE <3 )
DO IT BANDAI NAMCO <3

reviews.review_title: Do not buy if you are on quest2
reviews.review_description:
it's actually a really good interactive 3D movie
traveling through ship's corridor and hanger and seeing from inside mobile suit's cockpit is really an experience for gundam fan

BUT and it's a big but, if you are on quest2 be prepared for a lot of crash and freezes. I've watch for about 45min and had 4 crashes and freezes, it's clearly not optimize for quest2
I want to play a game, not watch anime. Why are there all these dialogues and I can't skip them? It's indescribably annoying. My glasses are draining and I've only encountered one or two enemies!!!!!

5 out of 5 for the immersive story. Looking forward to more content like this.

2 out of 5 for the unskippable, frustrating, and buggy interactive moments that interrupt the movie.

Was this game / experience even tested inside a headset???

Quest 3, my god the stutter / juddering when moving your hands or looking around or even just stayin perfectly still and the scene is moving is horrendous and god help anyone who suffers from motion sickness.

Is there zero optimisation in this??

Ive never seen a game that has a full cartoon look stutter with every single movement made!

The experience itself feels like your trying to watch a cartoon and someone keeps switchin your camera randomly.

You will be looking at something and then for no reason, and completely random it switches to somewhere else in the same scene so now you need to change the direction you was looking!

This along with ut loading in between every part is insane.

I got into the mech start up section and thought THIS is what the game needed to be, even THEN sat in the chair it changed camera, now im looking at me, then it hanges to looking at me from a DIFFERENT angle, then your back in 1st person again.

Such a weird design choice.

Was really looking forward to this but unfortunately it will be refunded shortly.

developer_response.body:
Hi  BeatzCreations,

First off, thanks so much for reaching out and being so upfront with your feedback. I’m really sorry to hear that your experience with “Mobile Suit Gundam: Silver Phantom” on the Quest 3 hasn’t been smooth. It sounds frustrating, especially with the stutters and the camera issues you mentioned—totally not what we want any player to go through.

We just rolled out an update yesterday (Version 1.0.23.152), which focuses a lot on enhancing performance and stability.

It’d mean a lot to us if you could give the game another shot with the new update. However, if you choose to go ahead with the refund, I totally get it. Either way, your feedback is super valuable and directly helps us make the necessary tweaks.

Thanks again for your honesty—it helps us do better. Hope to get this sorted out for you soon!

Jordan

reviews.review_description: It’s ground breaking in interactive storytelling. A few thing could have been better but story and game play was just amazing ￼

reviews.review_title: Anime Film where YOU are the Hero
reviews.review_description: This is essentially an interactive Gundam anime film where you are in the spacesuit of the main character - there is action, there are great sequences - come to this expecting a STORY more than a GAME and you will not be disappointed. As a fan of this franchise since the days of Gundam Wing on Toonami, I absolutely loved this and will do it again soon to experience alternate endings to the story.

reviews.review_title: JUST LET ME SKIP THE CUT SCENES!
reviews.review_description: LET ME SKIP THE CUT SCENES THEY’RE BORING!

reviews.review_title: The interactive elements let it down
reviews.review_description:
Even the launch screen had me excited as a fan. Loved the intro and the story was not bad. Like other comments I have seen not sure why it needs to jump around every 15 seconds to give you a different angle. 

All that said in the main I think the immersion in a world I have always loved was really special. 

My big issue with it is just how bad the interactive elements were, I can’t say I enjoyed the controls. I think the idea has potential but just poorly executed. 

I would (like so many) love to just sit and enjoy a deeply immersive viewing experience rather than have any of the interactive elements which would also allow them to move through some of the storytelling a bit more swiftly.
You must experience this.

reviews.review_title: This was the bees knees sieg zeon
reviews.review_description: Victory to zeon what a ball seeing a childhood favorite come to life was certainty a hoot if your a fan it’s a must see and if not don’t shy away there’s a nice walk into the Gundam universe to catch you up to speed even if your not a fan I see no reason one couldn’t enjoy this novel experience certainly hope to see more Gundam stuff in the future controls did get a tad wonky at a couple spots but the way it’s delivered makes up for the occasional miss bang up job can’t wait to see what’s next!

The controls are a little clunky, but overall a fun experience. The story is well told and doesn't feel cheap if you were worried about that, it's a full experience. There's even multiple endings! I haven't played with the AR modes yet, but if you were interested in a nice little side story its worth a look.

I wouldn't mind more, it's a very good first attempt.
